name: Subsurface Surveyor
reasoning: V1 best matches the prompt with a lean scout in a proper crouching pose at a cavern entrance, one hand steadying against stone, holding a torch with alert downward gaze. The composition is clean and readable at card size with excellent geometric framing. V2 has an awkward hand position (fingers splayed unnaturally on the ground) and shows a magical/tech device rather than rope at the belt. V3 depicts a female character with exaggerated features and lacks the practical scout aesthetic; the hand position is also anatomically strained.

artifacts_found.issue: Unnatural hand splaying and finger positioning on ground; magical/tech device instead of rope coils
Exaggerated facial features and body proportions; strained hand/arm anatomy in crouching position
